description: Guidde provides an AI-powered platform for creating and sharing instructional videos for software workflows.

build_patterns.name: Continuous-learning Flywheels
evidence: we'll always display playbooks based on their view count and ranking, which will change organically over time
automatically labels each one of the playbooks
personalizes the playbooks that are presented to you according to labels, your role, your geo-location and more
auto-updates across platforms
content recommendations
build_patterns.description: Guidde surfaces and ranks content using usage signals (views/rankings) and metadata (role, geo, app). These signals and the platform's automated tagging/labeling form a feedback loop: captured content is indexed and surfaced, user interactions inform ranking and recommendations, and popular/updated content is prioritized—driving continual improvement in what is shown and prompting more content capture and refinement.

build_patterns.name: Guardrail-as-LLM (Safety & Compliance Layer)
evidence: Blur sensitive info instantly and automatically
Guidde is SOC 2 Type II certified and supports SSO, role-based access, and global delivery
permission levels within the app to be set for your teammates
All data sent to or from Guidde is encrypted in transit using 256 bit encryption
we produce audit logs for all activity and use GCP for archival purposes
build_patterns.description: The product includes automated privacy/compliance checks applied to captured content (automatic blurring of sensitive info) plus RBAC, SSO, audit logs and encryption. This implies a guardrail tier that detects PII/sensitive fields (likely via models or heuristics) and enforces access/compliance policies before storing or surfacing content.

build_patterns.name: Micro-model Meshes
evidence: AI automatically produces a polished step-by-step video with synced narration and text
adds voiceover, captions, highlights, and multi-language options automatically
Guidde understands and transcribes what's being said and shown in your video content
Choose from over 100 voices and languages for perfect narration
Easily repurpose Zoom recordings, presentations, and YouTube content into controlled, searchable walkthroughs
build_patterns.description: Multiple specialized models are implied: ASR/transcription, TTS/voice synthesis, translation/localization, summarization/step extraction, and likely computer-vision/UI-detection for segmenting screen recordings. Those task-specific models are composed into a pipeline (mesh) to transform raw recordings into searchable, localized, narrated video SOPs.

build_patterns.name: Vertical Data Moats
evidence: turn NetSuite expertise into on-demand video guides inside the platform
turn your team's Workday expertise into searchable video libraries
Every organization uses SAP differently — Guidde lets subject-matter experts capture those workflows
Capture process knowledge once and reuse it across teams, departments, and regions
playbooks live on-app for posterity
build_patterns.description: Guidde captures company- and product-specific process videos and metadata, creating proprietary corpora of organizational know-how. Those collections (NetSuite, SAP, Workday playbooks) form a vertical/tenant-specific dataset that is hard for competitors to replicate and can be leveraged for tailored search, recommendations, and possibly fine-tuning.

build_patterns.name: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
evidence: searchable text
Guidde understands and transcribes what's being said and shown in your video content
automatically labels each one of the playbooks according to the app, the location, topics or features it's relevant for
search for playbooks the same way you search Google
AI-Generated Descriptions Automatically generate step-by-step descriptions
build_patterns.description: The product clearly indexes/transcribes videos and supports search and auto-generated descriptions. While explicit mention of vector stores or embeddings is absent, the functionality suggests a retrieval layer that returns relevant playbooks and may be used to contextualize or summarize content (i.e., a light RAG pattern: retrieval of indexed segments + generation of descriptions/summaries).

build_patterns.name: Knowledge Graphs / Metadata Indexing
evidence: automatically labels each one of the playbooks according to the app, the location, topics or features it's relevant for
personalizes the playbooks that are presented to you according to labels, your role, your geo-location and more
multi-tenant spaces approach where each space is fully isolated across companies and teams
Permissions can be set to include app settings, billing, user data or the ability to send or edit messages
build_patterns.description: There is heavy use of structured metadata, labels and role-based access that could be implemented as a permission-aware graph or metadata index tying users, roles, apps, locations and playbooks together. The text implies such relational indexing but does not explicitly describe a graph database or entity relationship layer.

build_patterns.name: Agentic Architectures
evidence: send a video request to your subject matter experts... The right person in your team will immediately get notified, create a new playbook and send it straight back to your inbox
opens up exactly where it's needed and can be watched while using the app
build_patterns.description: There is workflow automation and notifications (human-in-the-loop orchestration) but no clear evidence of autonomous agents that plan, chain tools, or take multi-step actions autonomously. The system orchestrates tasks but appears primarily event-driven with human authorship.

build_patterns.name: Natural-Language-to-Code
evidence: AI-Generated Descriptions Automatically generate step-by-step descriptions
Guidde instantly turns them into step-by-step, narrated walkthroughs
build_patterns.description: The content highlights automated description/summarization of recorded workflows but does not indicate converting natural language into executable rules, scripts or code. This pattern is largely absent.

sub_vertical: ERP user enablement and in-app training for enterprise software suites
sub_sub_vertical: in-app AI-powered walkthroughs for ERP/HRIS/CRM (NetSuite, SAP S/4HANA, Workday, etc.)

signals: SOC 2 Type II certification and security controls
SSO, role-based access, and multi-language delivery
Data encrypted in transit (256-bit) and at rest (AES-256)
Regular security practices: penetration testing, audit logs, disaster recovery
Hosting on Google Cloud Platform with multi-tenant spaces and isolated projects
Guidde Create offering AI-generated, narration-enabled video documentation
Guidde Broadcast and in-app guidance to surface in-workflow assistance

team_strengths: AI-driven, in-workflow guidance embedding across ERP/HR/CRM platforms
In-product video documentation platform enabling rapid content creation and localization
Security and compliance posture (SOC 2 Type II, SSO, 2FA, encryption, audit logs)
team_red_flags: No disclosed founders or leadership bios in available data; reliance on marketing materials for credibility
Limited public information about team scale and exact engineering leadership

pricing_evidence: Free plan with usage limits; upgrade to paid plan
No credit card required to start Guidde Create
Usage-based unit limits mentioned (units usage limit under our free plan)

evidence: In-app, context-specific guidance embedded in ERP/workflow apps (NetSuite, Workday, SAP)
Guidde Create allows self-serve creation of content; no IT escalation
"Guidde Broadcast works everywhere you do" indicates distribution across apps and ecosystems
Case studies and enterprise customers; content marketing with success stories

revenue_model.monetization_approach: subscription licenses with usage-based tiers; enterprise pricing via negotiation; in-app add-ons

distribution_advantages: Deep integrations with major ERP/HR/CRM platforms (NetSuite, SAP, Workday) enabling in-context guidance
In-app, context-aware video guidance eliminating context-switching
AI-driven content creation enabling rapid content refresh and consistency

acquisition_channels: content marketing with case studies and testimonials
customer_proof_points: Emerson case study (ERP training across 150 countries) showing 80% reduction in documentation time
Perforce case: significant reduction in video production time
FloQast case: accelerated content creation workflow
SentinelOne case: reduced content creation time

stage_evidence: Multiple enterprise case studies (Emerson, Nezasa, Perforce, SentinelOne, FloQast) showing real-world deployments
SOC 2 Type II certification, SSO, 2FA, multi-language delivery
Global deployment claims and support across large organizations (e.g., Emerson across 150 countries)
Embedded, in-context video SOPs across major ERP/CRM platforms (NetSuite, SAP, Workday, Ariba, Concur, SAP Ariba, SAP ERP)
Guidde Create with AI-generated narration, auto translation, and auto-updates; extensive integration ecosystem

core_features: AI-powered video SOP creation from screen capture (Guidde Create)
In-context, bite-sized video guidance embedded inside enterprise apps
One-click capture; no video editing required
Embedding in popular tools (Confluence, Salesforce, Zendesk, LMS, Notion, SharePoint)
Security features: SOC 2 Type II, SSO, 2FA, encryption at rest/in transit
differentiating_features: ADAP concept: AI Digital Adoption Platform vs traditional DAPs
Content capture once, update everywhere across ecosystems
Guidde Broadcast for distribution across platforms
Global, multi-workspace content segregation for large organizations
roadmap_signals: Expansion across key ERP/CRM ecosystems (NetSuite, SAP, Workday, SAP Ariba/Concur)
Enhanced AI-driven content updates and broader localization capabilities
Expanded distribution channels (Guidde Broadcast, deeper LMS and collaboration tool integrations)
Broader applicability to customer onboarding and external training scenarios

use_cases.primary_use_case: In-context, AI-generated video SOPs to standardize and accelerate ERP/CRM workflows and onboarding
secondary_use_cases: Onboarding new hires and enabling self-service training
Internal knowledge capture and process documentation
Customer onboarding and reducing support tickets
Content creation automation for policy/process guidance
customer_stories: Emerson case study: 80% reduction in documentation time across 150 countries; improved onboarding and reconciliation
Nezasa: Mastering Customer Onboarding with Guidde
Perforce: 90% reduction in video production time

product_risks: Content maintenance risk: processes change with releases; risk content becomes outdated if not continuously updated (though claims auto-updates exist)
Reliance on SMEs for initial content creation; potential bottlenecks if SMEs are unavailable
AI-generated narration quality and translation accuracy may require human review
API maturity and developer support not explicitly disclosed; potential integration friction
Security/compliance risks in content hosting and data exposure if not properly restricted
product_strengths: In-context, role-specific guidance directly within workflows
AI-driven content creation and localization at scale
Wide integration ecosystem within enterprise tools
Strong security posture (SOC 2 Type II, SSO, encryption, 2FA)
Rapid content creation and updates (minutes, not days/weeks)
Global, multi-language support and scalable deployment
Evidence from real customer outcomes (Emerson, Nezasa, Perforce, SentinelOne, FloQast)

unique_findings: End-to-end screen-capture → polished SOP pipeline: Guidde is not just hosting videos — they claim an automated pipeline that ingests raw screen/voice recordings (and uploads like Zoom), runs ASR, segments the recording into discrete steps, extracts UI actions, generates step-by-step text, produces captions and synthetic voiceovers, then renders a synchronized narrated video with highlights and annotations. That orchestration across CV/OCR, ASR, LLM-level instruction extraction and TTS is a nontrivial, integrated engineering stack.

Contextual, role-aware in-app surface: they emphasize surfacing bite-sized, role-specific guidance directly inside enterprise apps (NetSuite, SAP, Workday). That implies per-app connectors or an overlay/extension that can detect user role, current screen/context, and map the right playbook — a much deeper integration than typical iframe-embeds or KB links.

Automatic PII blurring at capture-time: Guidde claims automatic and instant blurring of sensitive information in videos. Practically this requires frame-level OCR + named-entity recognition (NER) or pattern detection (SSNs, credit card numbers, customer names) and either on-device or tightly controlled cloud processing to avoid exposing sensitive data — a high-difficulty feature for enterprise compliance.

Multi-channel distribution + auto-updates: videos are created once and automatically distributed/updated across many endpoints (LMS, Confluence, Zendesk, in-app). That means a content packaging/versioning system, webhooks/connector adapters per destination, and a canonical source of truth with update propagation and deprecation logic.

Multi-tenant, space-isolation architecture: they call out a multi-tenant spaces model with full isolation for companies/teams. For a video-first product that stores screen captures (potentially with PII), isolation at storage, access controls, RBAC, audit logs, and per-space keys/encryption are essential — an architectural choice oriented toward large enterprises.

Change detection and content recommendations: marketing mentions automated content recommendations and updates in minutes vs. days. To do that at scale requires either heuristics or ML to detect UI changes (DOM diffs for web, CV-based detection for non-web) and suggest re-records or automated patching of step text — a complex feedback loop that surfaces non-obvious engineering work.

Handling heavily customized ERPs/CRMs: their target (NetSuite, SAP, Workday) are highly customized per customer. Creating step-aligned guidance that remains valid requires robust methods to localize and normalize UI elements (mapping labels/fields across instances), which is a thorny engineering and ML problem seldom solved by simple screen-record pipelines.

Implicit dataset and model opportunity: by capturing thousands of annotated process recordings paired with structured step metadata, Guidde likely accumulates a unique dataset linking UI sequences → human narration → canonical SOP text. That dataset could be used to fine-tune models that perform more accurate step extraction, UI element identification, and auto-localization — a defensible technical asset if properly retained.

story_angles.headline: How Guidde stitches real-time, in-app video SOPs into NetSuite, SAP and Workday without breaking workflow
story_angles.summary: Behind the polished UX is an architecture that captures user workflows in-browser, runs AI transforms, and surfaces context-aware video guidance inside heavy enterprise apps. Engineers will want to know how Guidde balances client-side capture, secure server-side processing, low-latency in-app delivery, and role-aware access controls across multi-tenant customers.
evidence: Guidde uses a browser extension and desktop app to record workflows and embeds guidance directly inside NetSuite, SAP, Workday and other tools.
Guidde is SOC 2 Type II certified, supports SSO and role-based access, and hosts services on Google Cloud Platform with per-space isolation.

story_angles.headline: Their secret weapon: company‑specific workflow recordings as a vertical data moat for retrieval‑augmented guidance
story_angles.summary: Guidde accumulates labeled, role-specific video+text pairs that map exact UI states to narrated procedures — a potentially unique corpus for RAG, indexing, and personalized help. The real story isn't just 'video' but how those workflow traces become searchable embeddings, metadata graphs and recommendation signals that let ADAP surface the right clip at the right moment.
evidence: Guidde turns subject-matter-expert screen captures into searchable video SOPs with synced narration and text that surface in-context in ERP apps.

They claim automated generation of step-by-step descriptions, captions, multi-language voiceovers, and auto-updates across platforms — all of which imply indexed, structured metadata behind the scenes.

story_angles.headline: Why Guidde doubled down on video-first ADAP when the market went all-in on tooltip click‑tours
story_angles.summary: Most DAPs focused on fragile, linear click tours; Guidde bets on capturing real human workflows as short videos and using AI to make them actionable. For builders, the contrarian bet raises questions: is video-plus-RAG more robust to UI churn than click scripts, and does it scale without centralized content teams?
evidence: Guidde contrasts 'Video SOPs + searchable text' with 'Tooltip-based click tours', positioning video as visual, action-based and resilient to dynamic ERP workflows.
They emphasize minutes-to-create content by SMEs rather than days/weeks from centralized teams, signaling a distributed content model instead of centralized scripting.

story_angles.headline: Claim vs reality: Guidde promises 11x faster content creation — can that operationally cut month‑end chaos?
story_angles.summary: Guidde advertises dramatic efficiency gains (11x faster content creation, 80% drop in documentation time, fewer support tickets), but technical scrutiny is needed: how are accuracy, freshness and compliance validated at scale, and what SLOs/backups protect against stale or misleading guidance inside critical finance workflows?
evidence: Marketing claims include '11x Faster Content Creation' and Emerson reporting an 80% reduction in documentation time with perfect usefulness ratings.
Guidde offers features like auto-blurring sensitive info, multi-language voiceovers, and auto-updates across embedded platforms — all positioned as time-savers for creators and users.

anti_patterns.description: The product reads like a thin orchestration layer that stitches together screen recording, TTS/translation, and embed capabilities driven by third‑party AI and cloud infra rather than proprietary core models or IP.
evidence: Repeated claims of AI-generated voiceovers, captions, and translations without disclosure of proprietary models: "Guidde Create lets your NetSuite experts record a process once, and AI automatically produces a polished step-by-step video with synced narration and text."

Feature set focuses on automation of common capabilities (record, transcribe, TTS, blur sensitive info, multi-language) that can be implemented using widely available APIs and cloud services: "Add AI-generated voiceovers, blur sensitive info automatically, and localize in 40+ languages".
No technical detail on unique model training, proprietary datasets, or novel ML components; LLMs listed as 'unknown' in context.

anti_patterns.description: Core value (embedded, AI-processed video SOPs) could be positioned as a single feature that incumbents (LMS, DAP vendors, or platform providers) can add to their offerings, making it vulnerable to being absorbed.
evidence: Messaging compares Guidde to incumbents and frames the advantage as video + embed vs tooltip tours — a capability incumbent platforms can add: "ADAP Traditional DAPs Video SOPs + searchable text Tooltip-based click tours".

Frequent callsout of embedding into existing tools and LMSs: "Embed videos in Confluence, Salesforce, Zendesk, your LMS, or wherever work gets done" — suggests the product is additive rather than a system of record.
Prominent integrations and embedding are core to value; if those become standard features in major suites, differentiation declines.

anti_patterns.description: There is limited evidence of a defensible technical moat — no mention of proprietary, company‑specific training data, verticalized models, unique knowledge graphs, or network effects that produce a lasting advantage.
evidence: Claims focus on speed and convenience rather than unique algorithims or exclusive datasets: "Minutes to create and update" and "11x Faster Content Creation" without describing how.
No description of proprietary retrieval systems, knowledge graphs, or continual learning from vast customer corpora that would form a vertical data moat.
Tech stack and LLM provenance are unspecified; absence of RAG/metadata/indexing description limits argument for differentiated capabilities.

anti_patterns.description: Marketing-heavy language and broad, strong claims about outcomes (fast onboarding, huge reductions in tickets) are presented without supporting metrics methodology or technical transparency.
evidence: Broad effectiveness claims: "New hires become SAP-proficient in days, not weeks" and "Documentation time dropped 80%" (Emerson case) presented without detailed measurement context.

Frequent use of buzzwords and superlatives: "first-of-its-kind AI Digital Adoption Platform (ADAP)", "capture know-how at unprecedented speeds" and "AI automatically produces a polished step-by-step video".
Multiple generic ROI statements: "fewer support tickets", "faster onboarding" without quantified baselines or methodology.

anti_patterns.description: Position sits in a crowded space (digital adoption, in-app guidance, video training) and while video-first is a differentiator, the core components are common and competing vendors already target the same enterprise pain points.
evidence: Frequent comparisons to DAPs and LMSs and statements like "Your LMS, knowledge base, help center, and internal portals aren’t going anywhere" imply overlap with many existing categories.
Feature list (recording, auto-transcription, embedding, translations, SSO, RBAC, SOC2) matches typical enterprise checklist and does not on its face show a unique technical angle.
Market claim of broad applicability across NetSuite, Workday, SAP, etc. — useful but also where many incumbents already focus.

competitors.name: WalkMe
competitors.similarity: Digital adoption platform (in-app guidance) targeting enterprise software adoption and support
competitors.how_different: Guidde is video-first and AI-driven — it captures SME workflows as narrated videos, auto-edits and localizes them in minutes and embeds them contextually inside complex ERP/HR/CRM apps. WalkMe focuses on tooltip/step guides and rule-based flows rather than polished, searchable video SOPs.

competitors.name: Whatfix
competitors.similarity: Digital adoption and in-app guidance for enterprise apps (including SAP, Workday, Salesforce) and focus on improving onboarding and reducing support tickets
competitors.how_different: Whatfix emphasizes interactive click-through walkthroughs and automation of flows; Guidde emphasizes capturing real expert actions as short video playbooks with AI-generated narration, searchable transcripts, auto-blur and multi-language voiceovers — positioned for complex, dynamic ERP workflows rather than linear UI tours.

competitors.name: Pendo
competitors.similarity: In-app guidance and product analytics to drive adoption and feature usage
competitors.how_different: Pendo provides analytics-first tooling and lightweight in-app messaging and guides. Guidde provides producer-focused, AI-automated video documentation that is embedded into the workflow and optimized for teaching processes (esp. multi-step ERP tasks) rather than analytics-driven banners/tooltips.

competitors.name: Appcues
competitors.similarity: User onboarding and in-app guidance aimed at improving adoption and onboarding flows
competitors.how_different: Appcues is optimized for web-product onboarding flows and no-code creation of modals/tooltips. Guidde targets enterprise process documentation across complex tools (NetSuite, SAP, Workday) with a recorded-video playbook model and AI post-production, enabling SMEs to create content in minutes.

competitors.name: Spekit
competitors.similarity: Contextual learning and in-app enablement (notably strong in Salesforce/CRM enablement) and searchable knowledge in context of the app
competitors.how_different: Spekit focuses on CRM-specific microlearning and text-based playbooks inside the app. Guidde’s differentiator is narrated, step-by-step video playbooks that are auto-generated, localized and surfaced by role/context across many enterprise apps — more multimedia and less manual content authoring.

competitors.name: Loom
competitors.similarity: Screen + voice recording for quick how-to videos and asynchronous knowledge sharing
competitors.how_different: Loom is a general-purpose recorder for ad-hoc video messages and lacks built-in AI auto-editing, enterprise-grade in-app surfacing across ERP tools, role-based contextual delivery, and automated multi-language voiceovers and content tagging that Guidde offers.

competitors.name: Descript
competitors.similarity: AI-assisted video/audio editing, transcription and repurposing of recordings
competitors.how_different: Descript is focused on editing workflows and creators; Guidde adds a complete capture-to-distribution pipeline tailored to enterprise knowledge (in-app embedding, searchable playbooks, role personalization, auto-blur sensitive info, SOC2-grade multi-tenant controls).

competitors.name: Vidyard
competitors.similarity: Enterprise video platform for sales/training with hosting, analytics and integrations
competitors.how_different: Vidyard targets sales/marketing/video-hosting and interaction analytics; Guidde focuses specifically on process documentation and embedded, contextual video guidance inside operational enterprise apps with automated generation and localization.

competitors.name: Guru / Confluence / Notion (knowledge base providers)
competitors.similarity: Centralized knowledge storage, searchable documentation and integrations with enterprise apps
competitors.how_different: KB platforms are text-first and require manual authoring. Guidde layers continually updated, in-context video SOPs into those same workflows and can ingest existing recordings to auto-generate video playbooks with voiceovers, captions, and localization.

competitors.name: Apty / Userlane
competitors.similarity: Digital adoption platforms focused on enterprise app guidance and process compliance
competitors.how_different: Apty/Userlane provide in-app guidance and process analytics via step automation and monitoring. Guidde’s unique approach is SME-recorded video playbooks with AI-driven production and localization, prioritizing real-expert demonstrations over scripted click flows.

differentiation.primary: Video-first AI Digital Adoption Platform (ADAP) that captures subject-matter experts performing real workflows, then auto-produces polished, searchable, localized video SOPs and surfaces them in-context inside enterprise systems (ERP/CRM/HCM) — enabling minutes-to-create training vs days/weeks for traditional DAPs.
differentiation.technical: Automated pipeline: browser/desktop capture → AI transcription + step extraction → auto-editing (narration sync, captions, highlights) → auto-blur sensitive info → multi-language voiceovers/localization → metadata tagging and contextual indexing for in-app surfacing. Multi-tenant, space-isolated architecture, SOC 2 Type II, SSO, role-based access and integrations to embed videos inside target enterprise apps (NetSuite, SAP, Workday, HubSpot etc.).
differentiation.business: GTM focused on enterprise customers with complex, dynamic ERP/HR/finance workflows (NetSuite, SAP, Workday). Sells to enablement, operations, finance and HR teams as a way to reduce tickets, accelerate onboarding and lock-in process compliance. Offers rapid content creation (minutes), repurposing of existing recordings, and auto-updates across platforms — lowering the friction and cost of maintaining training content.
differentiation.positioning: Positions itself as the first/only 'AI Digital Adoption Platform (ADAP)' that captures real expertise as video and surfaces it in the flow of work, deliberately contrasting with traditional DAPs that rely on tooltip/click tours and centralized authoring teams. Emphasizes speed-to-content, contextuality inside complex enterprise systems, security and multi-language support.

secret_sauce.core_advantage: An integrated AI-driven capture-to-deliver pipeline that turns SME screen recordings into production-ready, role-personalized, localized video SOPs in minutes and then surfaces those videos contextually inside target enterprise applications.
secret_sauce.defensibility: Moderate — defensibility stems from a combination of: (1) customer-specific libraries of proprietary process videos (sticky content and high switching cost); (2) integrations and in-app surfacing for complex enterprise systems (implementation/configuration effort and relationships); (3) enterprise security/compliance (SOC 2, SSO, tenant isolation); and (4) operational expertise in automating transcription, step extraction, auto-blur and localization workflows. These together raise the bar for quick replication, though core AI techniques are broadly accessible.
evidence: "Guidde Create lets your NetSuite experts record a process once, and AI automatically produces a polished step-by-step video with synced narration and text."
"Minutes to create and update vs Days or weeks (Traditional DAPs)."
"11x Faster Content Creation" and "multi-language support" claims.
"SOC 2 Type II certified and supports SSO, role-based access, and global delivery."
"Multi-tenant spaces approach where each space is fully isolated across companies and teams."
$50,000,000 Series B funding (signals resources to build integrations, product and enterprise GTM).

competitive_analysis.moat_explanation: Guidde's moat is anchored in enterprise fit and content stickiness: once organizations build a library of role-specific, localized video SOPs embedded in their operational apps, switching costs rise. The company also leverages security/compliance (SOC 2), multi-tenant isolation and deep integrations with ERP/HCM systems which require setup and trust. The AI automation that accelerates content creation reduces churn risk and increases usage. However, core capabilities (screen capture, transcription, auto-editing) and in-app surfacing can be replicated by larger DAP vendors (WalkMe, Whatfix), video platforms (Loom/Descript) or new entrants, so the moat is not unassailable — it depends on execution, enterprise relationships, and growth of a customer-specific content network.

evidence_quotes: AI-powered video documentation that revolutionizes your content creation process
One-Click Capture Start recording with a single click and perform your workflow. Our AI does the rest.
AI-Generated Descriptions Automatically generate step-by-step descriptions
AI builds short, polished videos with step-by-step instructions that reflect your company’s exact way of working
Guidde AI Digital Adoption Platform (ADAP) vs Traditional Digital Adoption Platforms (DAPs)
Guiddes show up alongside the task at hand — whether that’s entering an invoice, approving an expense, or onboarding a new hire
Guidde understands and transcribes what's being said and shown in your video content
Hence it automatically labels each one of the playbooks according to the app, the location, topics or features it's relevant for
Add AI-generated voiceovers, blur sensitive info automatically, and localize in 40+ languages

Automated pipeline that converts screen recordings into synchronized narrated, captioned, and segmented video SOPs (single-click capture -> AI polish -> multi-language TTS) combining ASR, summarization and video editing automation.
Automatic sensitive-information blurring applied to captured videos (privacy-first video processing) integrated with RBAC and tenant isolation.

ADAP positioning: embedding contextual, role-specific video guidance directly into enterprise apps (NetSuite/Workday/SAP) rather than only offering tooltip-based click tours—delivering multimedia, in-context SOPs.
Cross-platform auto-update and embed propagation: single source of truth video assets that update across LMS, helpdesk, Confluence, and in-app surfaces.
Playbook surfacing driven by simple behavioral signals (view counts/ranking) plus metadata personalization (role, geo, app) to prioritize content organically.
